programme.desc: Deed encourages a friend to say ‘plane' and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different types of planes - a flying plane, a landing plane, a taking off plane, and a going on holiday plane. There's a fast plane, some racing planes and even a dancing plane!

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three does magic tricks with apples to show the others who goes first, who is biggest and how to surprise number friends

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Snowden has lost his pine cone ears and he can't hear a thing!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Teletubbyland Dipsy is King. Tinky Winky delivers Tubby Custard and Tubby Toast to King Dipsy - whatever he wants! Luckily for Tinky Winky they soon swap places

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Jo's dad can't fit his truck through a tunnel. Can Team Tinpo help?

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble refuses to climb a mountain, until Flicky Flack helps him forget how high it is

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy loves his fluffy jacket so much that he decides to keep it on inside. Mum says he doesn't need it, but Pablo knows his fluffy jacket needs him or it will feel lonely

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo and his friends encourage young viewers to question the world around them

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Observational documentary on the lives of reception children at two contrasting primary schools - one rural, and one urban

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Two new pirates are shipwrecked on the island and want to replace Cook and Line

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: A simple matter of duckling-sitting turns into a potential disaster when the ducklings escape into the wood

programme.title: Maddie, the Zoo and You
programme.desc: Maddie discovers how different animals move in different ways and learns about grip.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The rare Black Lion Tamarins need help to survive in the pristine forests of Sao Paulo.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie forgets her reading book, so Waffle takes it to her PE class at school

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran busts a groove when she teaches JoJo the moves to a traditional Saint Lucian dance. JoJo finds that learning a dance can take time, until she realises she can put her own twist on it.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The youngster and her duck try to find out why their friend Donkey seems sad

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Pepi Nana, Moon Baby and their friends discover the toy roundabout in the Toy House. In Storyland, the vehicles are running out of juice, but Dibillo has a juice station

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Cloud has been on holiday in space, but when it returns the Clangers notice it is very cold. Mother Clanger is worried when she sees it snowing rather than raining, and she calls for Major Clanger to come and have a look. The Clangers have never seen snow before. Major Clanger finds it very interesting and decides to take a sample to his workshop to analyse, but he's amazed to see that it has disappeared and has been replaced by water. While Small Clanger and Baby Soup Dragon enjoy a snowball fight, Tiny Clanger spots The Cloud shivering and comes up with a clever plan. Will Tiny's plan be enough to stop The Cloud shivering?

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy decides to kiss everything in the garden, but the Ninky Nonk feels left out. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Members of the England Illegal Money Lending team launch an early-morning operation against suspected loan sharks, and share one of the strangest places they have found hidden cash

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special show to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain. Charlie Ross visits the RAF Museum and pays his respects to pilots at the Battle of Britain Memorial

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama set during the Second World War, following the lives and loves of women who decide to serve their country by joining the Women's Land Army. New girl Connie Carter causes mayhem from the moment she arrives, Finch desperately tries to save his beloved pet pig, and a visit from a wealthy American industrialist spells intrigue at the Manor House. Starring Becci Gemmell, Jo Woodcock and Selene Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore what Britons ate during wartime and discover how some of the UK's favourite dishes have evolved from rationing. The duo also meet army cooks to find out what troops on the front line eat today, and prepare rabbit stew and carrot cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a sociable couple find a country home in Bedfordshire with enough room for them, their dogs and their very own bespoke garden pub, on a £500,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, tackling a wooden trunk, a memorial trophy and a gyroscopic toy cyclist

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle packs as much as she can, having told Gray she is going on a last-minute holiday with her family - but he remains suspicious. Tina and Frankie grow closer when they go for a drink at the Vic after work. But why is Frankie so interested in the Carters? Max is happy his divorce from Rainie has finally come through and thanks Ian for helping him out, telling him to keep £2,000 of the money he owes him. The rest, however, he wants back now. Ben takes Callum's advice and asks Phil what to do about the situation with Danny

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the reality of life in Britain's biggest special school. In the first episode pupils face their fears at an outdoor adventure centre. The focus of the trip is for the young people to have an opportunity to challenge their disability physically, and abseiling in a wheelchair epitomises this experience

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Idris Elba proves he is a dab hand at everything, Scribbler P returns for a lesson on blackface, and Mozart invents a new hit

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: Celebrity game show presented by the viral comedian. Featuring prank calls, exclusive house tours and lots of chaos. In this first episode, Filly is joined by EastEnders star Maisie Smith and her mum Julia. Contains some strong language

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster and journalist Emma Barnett and stand-up Tom Allen joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team assess properties about to be auctioned in Caerphilly in Wales, Bootle on Merseyside, and Stoke-on-Trent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Belfast show off a collection of designer goods they seized from a suspected paramilitary drug dealer, and a man who stole from his girlfriend has his assets sold off

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright reports on a Liverpool street with an infestation of vermin and a rented property above a row of shops that housing officers consider unsafe for habitation

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Bea falls for an Italian PoW, and Connie flirts with a bashful piano player while entertaining the troops. Meanwhile, Martin discovers he has detached retinas following his accident, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana snags herself a wealthy American admirer while helping Jack search for his grandson. Second World War drama, starring Jo Woodcock

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's fondness for picnics, preparing an alfresco lunch at Beaufort Polo Club in the Cotswolds. They also discover how the habit of outdoor dining in summer evolved into its current form, celebrate snacks such as Cornish pasties and Scotch eggs, and bake a Dundee cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple living in London's Docklands to find a home with some green space in Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills, and pays a visit to the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team tries to transform a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a tarnished rugby trophy, a hand-operated Victorian sewing machine, and a 1930s accordion, which has 64 individual components in need of repair

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: At the seaside, Mitch realises something is wrong with Chantelle, but rather than open up to her dad, she simply tells him she is missing the kids. Determined to help, he heads back to Walford to fetch them - but his plan soon backfires. Meanwhile, Ian makes a bid to become chairman of the council planning committee, and Ben fills Jay in on his plan to set up Danny

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Manchester single mothers Hayley and Megan join forces to help improve each other's diet, and turn to Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin for help in finding healthy meals all of their children will enjoy. Chris also visits a butcher to report on the importance of buying locally produced meat and asks members of the public what they think of some of the lesser known, cheaper cuts

programme.title: The Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Actor Ewan McGregor and his brother Colin, an RAF pilot, retell the story of the 1940 air battle that proved to be a turning point in the Second World War. The pair get hands-on experience of flying period fighter aircraft across the skies of England, giving Colin an opportunity to contrast his training in modern combat techniques with the skills required to master the Spitfire's less sophisticated technology. The duo also meet some of those who participated in the conflict itself

programme.title: Hell on Wheels
programme.desc: Changes to the team line-up have a devastating effect on morale, as defensive players who have learned to work well together find themselves split up. After 10 years of roller derby, team captain Kid is starting to think its time to hang up her skates, but emotions run high when she makes a surprise announcement after a victory against Birmingham

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Three more patients undergo treatment for skin conditions with the help of leading medical experts. Max was left with a scab on top of his head caused by sunburn six years ago, Eva suffers from a condition that causes excessive sweating, and Alix cannot bear to look at herself in the mirror because of her acne

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: The final batch of patients undergoing life-changing treatment for skin conditions share their stories. Hannah has had a severe case of eczema since she was two years old, Esiri suffers with hyperpigmentation that over-the-counter products have done nothing for, and Scot has become increasingly sensitive about his psoriasis over the course of his life

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two terraces near beautiful countryside in Prestatyn in north Wales and Darley Dale in the Peak District and a shop and flat in Folkestone in Kent are all sold under the hammer. All three properties need a lot of work and Martin Roberts, Dion Sublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new investors to hear their plans

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: National Crime Agency officers seize cash and drugs from a suspected drug dealer, and Border Force officers at Gatwick sell off a huge haul of gold seized from a passenger

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright heads to Liverpool where council housing officers deal with the case of a family who are struggling to cope with a landlord they claim is intrusive

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall give advice to two teams scouring Ardingly in West Sussex for items to sell at auction, where one of Willy Wonka's golden tickets goes under the hammer

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie discovers more about the piano player she is in love with. Joyce realises there is something wrong with Martin and decides to follow him. Elsewhere, Finch helps Esther raise money by setting up a boxing match with his old adversary Vernon. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Susan Cookson and Mykola Allen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a calligrapher from East London find a new home in Devon on a budget of £400,000, and attends a sea shanty festival on the southern coast

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich set to work transforming a garden in Bitterne, near Southampton, for Katie and Christian and their two children, Max and Olivia

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three treasured family heirlooms - a wooden toolbox, a plate camera and a marionette theatre

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating presents the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Entomologist Sarah Beynon and chef Andy Holcroft embark on a mission to bring insect-based food into the mainstream, promoting it as a more sustainable source of protein than meat. Before pitching to potential buyers, they go to the Netherlands to check that the insect farmers there can supply the quantity and quality of protein that they need and that it is safe for human consumption. Part of the Our Lives strand

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Shane Williams: Rugby, Concussion and Me
programme.desc: Shane Williams heads on a journey to understand how the world of rugby, from grass roots to the highest levels, is working to reduce concussions in the modern-day game and helping to make the sport safer for future generations. He talks to players past and present, the custodians of the game at World Rugby and leading global medical professionals studying the long-term effects of contact sport on the brain

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail forges a connection with Adil, a new visitor to base camp, and pushes a devastated Raelle to help him save a life. Tally uses Gerit as a distraction from her increasingly burdensome secrets, while Anacostia probes into Scylla's past

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The witches' training culminates at City Drop, their final training exercise. Devastating secrets come to light, splintering the unit and forcing Tally to question her blind trust. Later, a looming Spree threat changes the course of the cadets' lives

The pair get hands-on experience of flying period fighter aircraft across the skies of England, giving Colin an opportunity to contrast his training in modern combat techniques with the skills required to master the Spitfire's less sophisticated technology. The duo also meet some of those who participated in the conflict itself

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Three more patients undergo treatment for skin conditions with the help of leading medical experts. Max was left with a scab on top of his head caused by sunburn six years ago, Eva suffers from a condition that causes excessive sweating, and Alix cannot bear to look at herself in the mirror because of her acne

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: The final batch of patients undergoing life-changing treatment for skin conditions share their stories. Hannah has had a severe case of eczema since she was two years old, Esiri suffers with hyperpigmentation that over-the-counter products have done nothing for, and Scot has become increasingly sensitive about his psoriasis over the course of his life

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a mortgage advisor, a mother and daughter, and a print manager

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two terraces near beautiful countryside in Prestatyn in north Wales and Darley Dale in the Peak District and a shop and flat in Folkestone in Kent are all sold under the hammer. All three properties need a lot of work and Martin Roberts, Dion Sublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new investors to hear their plans

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: National Crime Agency officers seize cash and drugs from a suspected drug dealer, and Border Force officers at Gatwick sell off a huge haul of gold seized from a passenger

programme.desc: Matt Allwright heads to Liverpool where council housing officers deal with the case of a family who are struggling to cope with a landlord they claim is intrusive

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall give advice to two teams scouring Ardingly in West Sussex for items to sell at auction, where one of Willy Wonka's golden tickets goes under the hammer

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie discovers more about the piano player she is in love with. Joyce realises there is something wrong with Martin and decides to follow him. Elsewhere, Finch helps Esther raise money by setting up a boxing match with his old adversary Vernon. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Susan Cookson and Mykola Allen

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: A round-up of political news and events at Westminster and Holyrood.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood examines bogus Nectar cards, and fake fire resistant glass that was installed in schools, hospitals and churches around the UK

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Highlights of St Mirren v Celtic, which took place at St Mirren Park, and saw the hosts attempt to claim a first home victory over The Bhoys since January 2013

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail forges a connection with Adil, a new visitor to base camp, and pushes a devastated Raelle to help him save a life. Tally uses Gerit as a distraction from her increasingly burdensome secrets, while Anacostia probes into Scylla's past

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The witches' training culminates at City Drop, their final training exercise. Devastating secrets come to light, splintering the unit and forcing Tally to question her blind trust. Later, a looming Spree threat changes the course of the cadets' lives

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond. Members of the public have an hour to put their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters all explore mid-terraces as Martin Roberts visits Burton upon Trent, Dion Dublin takes a trip to Merseyside and Martel Maxwell is in Bromley, Kent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Members of the England Illegal Money Lending team launch an early-morning operation against suspected loan sharks, and share one of the strangest places they have found hidden cash

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets a Merseyside tenant in poor health who is trapped in a damp rented property, while a Birmingham resident has vanished into thin air - leaving their home empty

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A special show to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the Battle of Britain. Charlie Ross visits the RAF Museum and pays his respects to pilots at the Battle of Britain Memorial

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Drama set during the Second World War, following the lives and loves of women who decide to serve their country by joining the Women's Land Army. New girl Connie Carter causes mayhem from the moment she arrives, Finch desperately tries to save his beloved pet pig, and a visit from a wealthy American industrialist spells intrigue at the Manor House. Starring Becci Gemmell, Jo Woodcock and Selene Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore what Britons ate during wartime and discover how some of the UK's favourite dishes have evolved from rationing. The duo also meet army cooks to find out what troops on the front line eat today, and prepare rabbit stew and carrot cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a sociable couple find a country home in Bedfordshire with enough room for them, their dogs and their very own bespoke garden pub, on a £500,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson meet Esme and Richard, who live in Rugby with their seven pets and want an English country garden with areas for the animals and an outdoor cinema

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, tackling a wooden trunk, a memorial trophy and a gyroscopic toy cyclist

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Conor McKinney helps release rescued curlews, while Gavin Andrews helps clear rubbish from local rivers

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle packs as much as she can, having told Gray she is going on a last-minute holiday with her family - but he remains suspicious. Tina and Frankie grow closer when they go for a drink at the Vic after work. But why is Frankie so interested in the Carters? Max is happy his divorce from Rainie has finally come through and thanks Ian for helping him out, telling him to keep £2,000 of the money he owes him. The rest, however, he wants back now. Ben takes Callum's advice and asks Phil what to do about the situation with Danny

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: Our Weddings
programme.desc: A celebration of Northern Irish weddings from the past 60 years. Featuring contributions from couples of various generations who look back on their special day and the cultural influences they were inspired to emulate - from Princess Diana's record-breaking train to the Beatles' famous haircuts

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: The ex-footballer reflects on his upbringing in Belfast's Shankill district and his career at the top of his sport during the 1980s. Raised in Belfast's Shankill district, Norman went on to become the youngest player to score for Manchester United's first team, the youngest FA Cup scorer in history and the youngest ever footballer to play in World Cup finals. However, within a decade, his career was cut cruelly short. Speaking to Stephen Watson, Norman reveals the true story behind his sporting life - what it was like to have and then lose it all - and how he managed to rebuild his life. Featuring unseen archive material

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: Idris Elba proves he is a dab hand at everything, Scribbler P returns for a lesson on blackface, and Mozart invents a new hit

programme.title: Yung Filly's Celeb Lock-In
programme.desc: Celebrity game show presented by the viral comedian. Featuring prank calls, exclusive house tours and lots of chaos. In this first episode, Filly is joined by EastEnders star Maisie Smith and her mum Julia. Contains some strong language

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Comedian Rhod Gilbert takes over the reins for an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster and journalist Emma Barnett and stand-up Tom Allen joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in Belfast show off a collection of designer goods they seized from a suspected paramilitary drug dealer, and a man who stole from his girlfriend has his assets sold off

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright reports on a Liverpool street with an infestation of vermin and a rented property above a row of shops that housing officers consider unsafe for habitation

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Bea falls for an Italian PoW, and Connie flirts with a bashful piano player while entertaining the troops. Meanwhile, Martin discovers he has detached retinas following his accident, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana snags herself a wealthy American admirer while helping Jack search for his grandson. Second World War drama, starring Jo Woodcock

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's fondness for picnics, preparing an alfresco lunch at Beaufort Polo Club in the Cotswolds. They also discover how the habit of outdoor dining in summer evolved into its current form, celebrate snacks such as Cornish pasties and Scotch eggs, and bake a Dundee cake

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple living in London's Docklands to find a home with some green space in Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills, and pays a visit to the Henley Regatta

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team tries to transform a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a tarnished rugby trophy, a hand-operated Victorian sewing machine, and a 1930s accordion, which has 64 individual components in need of repair

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: At the seaside, Mitch realises something is wrong with Chantelle, but rather than open up to her dad, she simply tells him she is missing the kids. Determined to help, he heads back to Walford to fetch them - but his plan soon backfires. Meanwhile, Ian makes a bid to become chairman of the council planning committee, and Ben fills Jay in on his plan to set up Danny

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Manchester single mothers Hayley and Megan join forces to help improve each other's diet, and turn to Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin for help in finding healthy meals all of their children will enjoy. Chris also visits a butcher to report on the importance of buying locally produced meat and asks members of the public what they think of some of the lesser known, cheaper cuts

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.desc: One of the most important texts in the Scottish Gaelic language, the Book of Deer was written in the ancient monastery of Deer that disappeared over a thousand years ago. This is the story of a team of archaeologists and volunteers who spent nine days hunting for the lost monastery of Deer, where the book was written. In the event, those involved found more than they imagined possible

programme.desc: Billy reports his suspicions to the police, David hears from his house's insurers and Toyah answers tough questions concerning her past

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: The team picks up fat cat Tiger, who has a rather embarrassing problem down below, which means going on a diet, while nine-year-old greyhound Carter could have a serious underlying health problem. Nurse Charlotte has a particularly unusual patient to treat as a stray corn snake is brought in, while some cute kittens visit the mobile unit for vaccinations

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Asha sets out to clear the air with Dev, Oliver's doctor delivers a devastating verdict and David grows wary of Ray's motives

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Fact-based drama starring David Tennant as notorious serial killer Dennis Nilsen. On 9 February 1983, Detective Chief Inspector Peter Jay is called to the leafy north London address of 23 Cranley Gardens, where he discovers the drains clogged with rotting human flesh and bones. But the extent of this grisly discovery only becomes apparent when the culprit returns from work. Dennis Nilsen, a meek man, freely admits to the police that it's not just one or two bodies, but as many as 15 or 16. As the police try to secure a conviction and identify the victims from scant DNA evidence, biographer Brian Masters attempts to understand why. A game of chess with 'Des' begins, and while Brian may be exhilarated by his newest subject, he's unaware what this new obsession could do to his life. Also starring Daniel Mays, Jason Watkins and Barry Ward

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Captain Sir Tom Moore joins Piers Morgan to look back on his extraordinary life - which spans an entire century. He reflects on the devastating accident that left him in intensive care at the age of 98 - and how his gratitude to the NHS and battle to regain mobility led to the record-breaking charity walk which first brought him to public attention. Tom takes stock of his wartime service, reveals the untold story of his first marriage and discusses how his beloved second wife Pamela brought him happiness and a family before her terminal illness left him rebuilding his life once again

programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the daily work of Dyfed-Powys Police officers over the busy summer months. Dyfed-Powys is the smallest police force in the UK, but has to patrol the largest police area, their jurisdiction taking in national parks and a lengthy run of the spectacular South Wales coastline. In this first episode, officers in the seaside town of Tenby on the western side of Carmarthen Bay have to deal with rowdy holidaymakers causing trouble and track down a pensioner who has been reported missing

programme.title: Flying for Britain with David Jason
programme.desc: The actor meets members of the RAF's Battle of Britain Memorial Flight, charged with preserving historic aircraft from the Second World War. The team is emerging from lockdown, having been forced to cancel several anniversary events. A qualified pilot, David joins them in their historic Spitfires and Hurricanes for a special tribute fly-past

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Des withdraws his cooperation from the police just as Jay thinks they have what they need to convict him of all the murders. Instead, he begins confiding in Brian, leaving Jay faced with the challenge of having to deal with a criminal biographer for the first time in his career. Des sessions with Brian give him the chance to analyse his own behaviour, leading him to make the shocking decision to plead not guilty in court

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals

programme.desc: Reeling from Des' change in plea, Jay and the police throw themselves into proving premeditation, hoping to show to the court he was of sound mind when he committed his crimes. Could this seemingly open-and-shut murder investigation really be close to collapsing?

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Reeling from Des' change in plea, Jay and the police throw themselves into proving premeditation, hoping to show to the court he was of sound mind when he committed his crimes. programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Fact-based drama starring David Tennant as notorious serial killer Dennis Nilsen. On 9 February 1983, Detective Chief Inspector Peter Jay is called to the leafy north London address of 23 Cranley Gardens, where he discovers the drains clogged with rotting human flesh and bones. But the extent of this grisly discovery only becomes apparent when the culprit returns from work. Dennis Nilsen, a meek man, freely admits to the police that it's not just one or two bodies, but as many as 15 or 16. As the police try to secure a conviction and identify the victims from scant DNA evidence, biographer Brian Masters attempts to understand why. A game of chess with 'Des' begins, and while Brian may be exhilarated by his newest subject, he's unaware what this new obsession could do to his life. Also starring Daniel Mays, Jason Watkins and Barry Ward

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Captain Sir Tom Moore joins Piers Morgan to look back on his extraordinary life - which spans an entire century. He reflects on the devastating accident that left him in intensive care at the age of 98 - and how his gratitude to the NHS and battle to regain mobility led to the record-breaking charity walk which first brought him to public attention. Tom takes stock of his wartime service, reveals the untold story of his first marriage and discusses how his beloved second wife Pamela brought him happiness and a family before her terminal illness left him rebuilding his life once again

programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the daily work of Dyfed-Powys Police officers over the busy summer months. Dyfed-Powys is the smallest police force in the UK, but has to patrol the largest police area, their jurisdiction taking in national parks and a lengthy run of the spectacular South Wales coastline. In this first episode, officers in the seaside town of Tenby on the western side of Carmarthen Bay have to deal with rowdy holidaymakers causing trouble and track down a pensioner who has been reported missing

programme.title: Flying for Britain with David Jason
programme.desc: The actor meets members of the RAF's Battle of Britain Memorial Flight, charged with preserving historic aircraft from the Second World War. The team is emerging from lockdown, having been forced to cancel several anniversary events. A qualified pilot, David joins them in their historic Spitfires and Hurricanes for a special tribute fly-past

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Des withdraws his cooperation from the police just as Jay thinks they have what they need to convict him of all the murders. Instead, he begins confiding in Brian, leaving Jay faced with the challenge of having to deal with a criminal biographer for the first time in his career. Des sessions with Brian give him the chance to analyse his own behaviour, leading him to make the shocking decision to plead not guilty in court

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the royals' rollercoaster relationship with the press, beginning with the Queen's 1953-1954 Commonwealth tour, when cameramen worked hand-in-hand with the palace. A few years later, newspaper owner Lord Altrincham was attacked for daring to publish criticisms of the Queen, but by the mid-1970s Princess Margaret's hedonistic lifestyle was the subject of increasing press attention. But that was only the beginning. In the 1990s, the press has a field day over the breakdown of Charles and Diana's marriage, feeding on royal scandals

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones sets out to explore eight very disparate kinds of quintessentially British terrain, investigating how they have dictated different ways of living, working and playing - both in the past and as they are now - across the entire country, while also unearthing quirky and funny facts en route. He begins by heading to the South Downs, which are situated in Hampshire and East and West Sussex, taking to the air for a bird's eye view and attempting to get to know one of the region's woolliest inhabitants

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the picturesque estuaries of Suffolk and Essex in the second leg of his journey through the British landscape. Amongst his challenges are attempting to eat a 'Colchester Native', helping to build a replica of the Mayflower and enjoying the beauty of Constable country

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective and his team find themselves investigating the murder of one of their own. Cabbot is convinced there is a link between the deaths of Lucy Payne and her colleague, but is dismissed as trying to push her career ambitions. However, after receiving new information from another force, she begins to uncover the real connection between the killings. Andrea Lowe stars

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Recently single teacher Laura Nielson is set up with Andrew Earlham, a widowed surgeon and the father of one of her students. The two enjoy a first date, but the next day rumours and accusations begin to circulate and the two find themselves entangled in a web of deceit and confusion. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Shelley Conn, Zoe Tapper and Warren Brown

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike and Sally plan a holiday, but things run far from smoothly when Sid gets involved with the financial planning. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is still bitter about Kim inheriting Home Farm and the business. Dean's dad accuses Tony of assaulting his son. Sophie is sick of Zoe keeping their relationship a secret

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Colonel and Joan Maltravers host a dinner party to celebrate their son's return from Kenya but the meal is disrupted by an armed robbery. Guest starring Simon Williams, his wife Lucy Fleming and their son Tam Williams

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid revolts against the chairman of the local residents association

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The couple go to a family wedding, but they don't bargain on Jean's cousin Peggy being there

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A casino is robbed, presenting Frost with a fresh challenge to his deductive powers, and things go from bad to worse when a local copper is reported missing and a well-known armed thug is spotted in Denton. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander and George Anton

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jenny's behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy cannot face going to school. Chris takes out his problems on Sophie. The police question the Dingles about a robbery in the village. Biff provides Kathy with some comfort

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy tells Biff he saw him with Kathy, and Biff tries to tell Linda about the incident with Kathy. Becky attempts to get Dean's dad to drop the charges

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Platts return from Canada with a shock for Audrey. Jim stops drinking and tells Bill he wants to return to work. Des is horrified when Mavis gives Becky a budgie

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bill apologises to Maureen, thinking he has embarrassed her. Don is furious when he hears Nick is living in Canada. Tricia visits Carl in prison

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find his missing daughter. Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the perpetrator is found dead in his home and when a battered body is found in a local nightclub, the identity of the victim leads Banks and Cabbot to a devastating realisation about both cases

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's online attack on Andrew yields unexpected consequences as a figure from her past returns to haunt her, potentially jeopardising the case. Laura resorts to extreme measures to find hard evidence of Andrew's guilt

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean informs Sid she wants a lover to bring a bit of spice back into her life. Comedy, starring Diana Coupland and Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor Mildred's hopes are raised when work-shy George looks for a job, but his chances of finding gainful employment seem slim. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is determined not to have Andy put off going to school because of Viv's demonstration. Zoe asks Sophie to spy for her at Kim's party

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim hopes to be discreet about her relationship with Steve. Eric is jumpy when he spots Kathy looking through her accounts. Sophie tells Butch that he is being pathetic

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy moves out of number 11. Audrey convinces herself Nick is better off with Stephen than with Gail. The Duckworths comfort Tricia as she breaks down in tears over her black eye.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is surprised by Raquel's new positive attitude. Jim teases Bill about his relationship with Maureen. The police ask Tricia to press charges against Carl

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: David Jason returns as DI Jack Frost in this feature-length episode. Faced with the death of a police informant on an estate riven with racial tension, the detective finds his patience pushed to the limit. Bruce Alexander and Lennie James also star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. A surveillance operation establishes a clear link between the murders of Charles McKay and Barry Clough and the subsequent investigation takes a number of alarming twists before uncovering a conspiracy pointing to one of the team being an informer. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's worst fears come to pass and her hidden past is exposed, but she must fight to put her personal struggles aside for the sake of one of her pupils, whose physical distress throws her unexpectedly at the mercy of Andrew Earlham. Vanessa also finds her resolve shaken after an unsettling encounter with Andrew

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: David Jason returns as DI Jack Frost in this feature-length episode. Faced with the death of a police informant on an estate riven with racial tension, the detective finds his patience pushed to the limit. Bruce Alexander and Lennie James also star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle is upset when Paddy confronts her for starting a family feud and is also annoyed to hear Paddy and Marlon talking about her and Jamie behind their backs. As Harriet heads off to a religious retreat, Dawn tries to calm a paranoid Will, who refuses to listen and places an urgent furtive phone call

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn admonishes Will, reminding him what happened last time he brought a gun into the house, while Cain notices the church door has been left open and finds Harriet inside sobbing. When Amy arrives at the Woolpack for her pizza tasting night she's hurt to discover Marlon has also invited others, and things get worse when Tracy arrives as there's an obvious frostiness between the two girls

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken is asked to direct the school play after the drama teacher falls ill. Jim heckles Liz in the street over money. Tricia assures the Duckworths she's finished with Carl

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fred offers Alec the chance to join Square Dealers. Ken struggles directing A Midsummer Night's Dream. Maud is stunned when Maureen tells her about her blind date

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is furious that the police let Kim go without charging her. Andy and the Sugdens have a meeting with the headmistress. Kim makes Sophie doubt Frank's innocence

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The coffin containing the mystery body is exhumed. Sophie tells Kim that she needs to get to know James again. DI Cooke arrives just as Chris threatens Kim

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton and Walker investigate a security van raid in Ashfordly and are convinced there is a link to a recent robbery at a local airfield, but a senior officer visiting from Middlesbrough dismisses their claims. Vernon takes a sudden interest in the flower show when he discovers David might be the owner of a rare, potentially prize-winning fuchsia and turns to Blaketon and Ventress for help when the plant suddenly disappears

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid receives a birthday present from the family - but he does not want it. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike bring home a tramp, much to Sid's disgust. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' bed collapses and George finds himself short of cash to acquire a new one. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the crime drama. DI Frost has his hands full coping with death threats, arson and a staff shortage at the station - but anything is better than staying at home with an unwelcome guest. David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott

programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is determined not to have Andy put off going to school because of Viv's demonstration. Zoe asks Sophie to spy for her at Kim's party

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim hopes to be discreet about her relationship with Steve. Eric is jumpy when he spots Kathy looking through her accounts. Sophie tells Butch that he is being pathetic

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy moves out of number 11. Audrey convinces herself Nick is better off with Stephen than with Gail. The Duckworths comfort Tricia as she breaks down in tears over her black eye.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is surprised by Raquel's new positive attitude. Jim teases Bill about his relationship with Maureen. The police ask Tricia to press charges against Carl

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: David Jason returns as DI Jack Frost in this feature-length episode. Faced with the death of a police informant on an estate riven with racial tension, the detective finds his patience pushed to the limit. Bruce Alexander and Lennie James also star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. A surveillance operation establishes a clear link between the murders of Charles McKay and Barry Clough and the subsequent investigation takes a number of alarming twists before uncovering a conspiracy pointing to one of the team being an informer. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's worst fears come to pass and her hidden past is exposed, but she must fight to put her personal struggles aside for the sake of one of her pupils, whose physical distress throws her unexpectedly at the mercy of Andrew Earlham. Vanessa also finds her resolve shaken after an unsettling encounter with Andrew

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: David Jason returns as DI Jack Frost in this feature-length episode. Faced with the death of a police informant on an estate riven with racial tension, the detective finds his patience pushed to the limit. Bruce Alexander and Lennie James also star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid dodges a stage performance, only to end up sorting out props and scenery. Classic comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: World Cup Rivalries: England v Argentina
programme.desc: Short sports film about this compelling sporting rivalry.

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A foreign spy trained to kill and impersonate a top Canadian agent presents a problem for both the team and his employers. Crime drama, starring Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter track down those responsible for killing a driver and injuring a policeman while making off with vehicles carrying brandy on two separate occasions. Carter's new girlfriend, rookie cop Julie Kingdom, pursues a known fence and recovers the brandy. However, when Regan wants to take the credit, he and Carter come to blows. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An intruder begins breaking into people's homes and destroying their plaster statuettes of Napoleon, but leaving more valuable items untouched. Holmes resolves to find out why, eventually uncovering a twisted tale of theft and murder. Jeremy Brett stars as the master detective

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Enemy agents use a fiendish device to transfer their minds into the bodies of Steed and Mrs Peel - whose subsequent strange behaviour makes them a threat to national security. Freddie Jones and Patricia Haines guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur comes up with a plan to boost business at the Winchester Club by staging variety nights. The idea proves popular until the karaoke machine, rented from the fearsome Gabadini family, is stolen. George Cole stars

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle have reason to suspect their former mentor has turned 'rogue' for money after two key suspects in a drug-smuggling case are delivered to their killers. Action drama, with Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Glyn Owen

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 15, featuring a mountainous 174.5km course from Lyon to Grand Colombier. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: Harry and the Contessa agree to play along when Inspector Newman asks them to help protect a dead man. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Tony Anholt

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Suffering from nervous exhaustion, Holmes sets off for a break on the wild Cornish peninsula, where he hopes the peace and quiet will help him convalesce. Unfortunately, several murders ensure his holiday is far from relaxing. Period detective drama, guest starring Denis Quilley

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The brother of the late Clement Armstrong devises a plan to avenge his death at the hands of the cybernauts by setting a deadly trap for Steed and Emma. Peter Cushing guest stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur dabbles in the world of nouvelle cuisine, taking bookings on banquets. However, his chef goes on the run from a group of gangsters leaving him in the lurch when catering for a policeman's retirement party. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: As whole estates of houses begin to crumble, CI5 uncovers corruption surrounding the awarding of contracts to build homes. Maurice Denham and Bill Fraser guest star alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A member of a group of convicts slips off the roof and dies during a robbery. The apparent leader of the gang appears to be having doubts about his life and Regan senses he is deliberately pointing clues in a desire to be caught during his next job. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Gary Imlach presents a look back at the most recent stages as the race reached a rest day. Plus, reports from the riders' press conferences and a look ahead to the final days

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jeremy Wade takes on the biggest investigation of his career - to unravel what happened to the passengers of the RMS Laconia, torpedoed in the mid-Atlantic

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The team head to the Netherlands, which is renowned for its extensive waterways, but they will be focusing their attention on a lake called Carplantis which is home to some fantastic-looking, and very large carp. Plus, they also take in a spot of barbel fishing on the River Meuse

programme.title: Gone in 60 Seconds (2000)
programme.desc: A veteran car thief is forced out of retirement to steal 50 top-of-the-range vehicles in exchange for his brother's life. While his sibling is held hostage by a sadistic gangster, he asks his former cronies to help - but with the clock ticking and just a few days remaining to pull off the daunting task, their chances of success are hanging by a thread. Thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, Angelina Jolie, Giovanni Ribisi and Robert Duvall

programme.title: Smokey and the Bandit (1977)

Comedy, starring Burt Reynolds, Jackie Gleason, Sally Field and Jerry Reed

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: The sound of wedding bells and police sirens combine as crimebusting duo Riggs and Murtaugh attend Leo Getz's surprise wedding - with Murtaugh as Leo's best man. However, the duo soon find themselves at the heart of the action when fiancee Nina disappears at a key moment - with a very large sum of money apparently in her possession. Later, Riggs comes face to face with someone from his past, and Murtaugh receives news that seems likely to change his life for ever. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star
